Title: Arabidopsis acyl-CoA-binding protein ACBP3 participates in plant response to hypoxia by modulating very-long-chain fatty acid metabolism

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: very-long-chain fatty acid-containing phosphatidylserine and glucosylinositolphosphorylceramides
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with hypoxia tolerance and cellular ROS homeostasis under submergence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by dark submergence and light submergence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ACBP3 transcripts strongly induced under DS and LS; ACBP3pro:GUS activated by DS and LS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark submergence (DS); light submergence (LS); ethanol treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of hypoxia tolerance; overexpression reduces VLCFA-containing PS and GIPC accumulation and causes hypersensitivity to submergence/ethanol, while knockout or RNAi increases these lipids and enhances tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and knockout/RNAi phenotyping; qRT-PCR; lipid profiling by ESI-MS/MS and sphingolipid MS/MS; microscale thermophoresis acyl-CoA-binding assay; genetic interaction with myb30-1
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes hypoxia hypersensitivity, elevated H2O2 accumulation, and reduced induction of hypoxia-responsive genes
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics by ESI-MS/MS and sphingolipid profiling; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing ACBP3 expression may improve submergence/hypoxia tolerance through maintenance of VLCFA-derived membrane lipids, but possible pleiotropic effects on defense/senescence should be considered
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ACBP3 recombinant protein binds VLC acyl-CoA esters (20:0-, 22:0-, 24:0-CoA) with high affinity in vitro; paper concludes ACBP3 acts downstream of MYB30 in VLCFA-mediated hypoxia signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACBP3 is strongly induced by submergence under both dark and light conditions. 2. ACBP3 overexpression decreases hypoxia tolerance and lowers VLCFA-containing PS and GIPC species, whereas ACBP3 suppression has the opposite effect. 3. Recombinant ACBP3 preferentially binds VLC acyl-CoAs, supporting a role in VLCFA-related lipid metabolism during hypoxia.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: very-long-chain fatty acids and VLCFA-derived lipids including phosphatidylserine and glucosylinositolphosphorylceramides
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to hypoxia tolerance and stress-associated lipid homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of VLCFA-mediated hypoxia response; myb30-1 shows hypersensitivity to LS and ethanol, while ACBP3-RNAi suppresses this phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant phenotyping under LS and ethanol; double-combination analysis with ACBP3-RNAi; prior established function referenced for VLCFA biosynthesis regulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Described as a master regulator of VLCFA biosynthesis required for hypoxia response
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes encoding subunits/components of the acyl-CoA elongase complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates VLCFA biosynthetic genes of the acyl-CoA elongase complex
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Hypoxia-related stress context; previously linked to pathogen attack and ABA response
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of MYB30 increases sensitivity to hypoxia-related treatments
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing MYB30-regulated VLCFA biosynthesis may support submergence tolerance, though stress-defense and cell-death tradeoffs should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper infers ACBP3 acts downstream of MYB30 in VLCFA-mediated hypoxia signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB30 is presented as a master regulator of VLCFA biosynthesis. 2. The myb30-1 mutant is hypersensitive to light submergence and ethanol. 3. ACBP3-RNAi ameliorates the myb30-1 phenotype, linking MYB30-controlled VLCFA metabolism to ACBP3-mediated hypoxia response.
